findings.evidence: Lines 136-141 define `map : ... →ₐc[K] ...` and lines 208-220 only restate counit/comultiplication preservation; `rg -n "antipode.*map|map.*antipode|commute.*antipode" mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra code/TauCeti` finds no supporting antipode-compatibility lemma, while Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean lists uniqueness of antipodes as a TODO.
findings.fix: Either restrict the doc/API claim to bialgebra functoriality, or add the missing Hopf characteristic lemma, e.g. a `[simp]` theorem comparing `HopfAlgebraStruct.antipode K (map f x)` with `map f (HopfAlgebraStruct.antipode K x)` under Hopf hypotheses.
findings.issue: `BaseChange.map` is documented as the Hopf-algebra scalar extension of a morphism, but it only returns a `BialgHom` and the API has no lemma that it commutes with antipodes.

summary: The base-change object and Hopf-operation handles are mostly in place, but the public map API claims Hopf functoriality without exposing or supporting antipode compatibility.
